 166    def serialize_for_vscode(self)->str:

 82                "label": "{GeneralUtilities.escape_json_string_value(self.label)}",

 84                "type": "shell",
 85                "options": {{
 86                    {cwd}
 87                }},
 88                "aliases": [

 90                ],
 91                {desc}
 92                "allowcustomarguments": {str(self.allow_custom_arguments).lower()}
 93            }}"""
 094        return result

 0412        if (not GeneralUtilities.contains_line(result[1].splitlines(), f"gpg\\:\\ using\\ [A-Za-z0-9]+\\ key\\ [A-Za-z0-
 413            # TODO check whether this works on machines where gpg is installed in another langauge than english
 0414            return False
 0415        if (not GeneralUtilities.contains_line(result[1].splitlines(), "gpg\\:\\ Good\\ signature\\ from")):
 416            # TODO check whether this works on machines where gpg is installed in another langauge than english
 0417            return False
 0418        return True

 0432        if ignore_commits_which_are_not_in_history_of_head:

 0434        return result

 1440    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments

 1446            return True
 1447        elif exit_code == 1:
 1448            return False
 449        else:
 0450            raise ValueError(f'Can not calculate if {ancestor} is an ancestor of {descendant} in repository {repository_
 451

 0457            if GeneralUtilities.string_has_content(line):
 0458                return True
 0459        return False

 1554    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 1555    def git_pull(self, folder: str, remote: str, localbranchname: str, remotebranchname: str, force: bool = False) -> No
 0556        self.is_git_or_bare_git_repository(folder)
 0557        argument = f"pull {remote} {remotebranchname}:{localbranchname}"
 0558        if force:

 0560        self.run_program("git", argument, folder, throw_exception_if_exitcode_is_not_zero=True)

 1562    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 1563    def git_list_remote_branches(self, folder: str, remote: str, fetch: bool) -> list[str]:
 0564        self.is_git_or_bare_git_repository(folder)
 0565        if fetch:
 0566            self.git_fetch(folder, remote)
 0567        run_program_result = self.run_program("git", f"branch -rl {remote}/*", folder, throw_exception_if_exitcode_is_no
 0568        output = GeneralUtilities.string_to_lines(run_program_result[1])
 0569        result = list[str]()
 0570        for item in output:
 0571            striped_item = item.strip()
 0572            if GeneralUtilities.string_has_content(striped_item):
 0573                branch: str = None
 0574                if " " in striped_item:
 0575                    branch = striped_item.split(" ")[0]
 576                else:
 0577                    branch = striped_item
 0578                branchname = branch[len(remote)+1:]
 0579                if branchname != "HEAD":
 0580                    result.append(branchname)
 0581        return result
 582
 1583    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments

 0589            if include_submodules:

 0592            if mirror:
 0593                args.append("--mirror")
 0594            self.run_program_argsasarray("git", args, os.getcwd(), throw_exception_if_exitcode_is_not_zero=True)
 595

 1656    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 1657    def git_commit(self, directory: str, message: str = "Saved changes.", author_name: str = None, author_email: str = N
 658        """no_changes_behavior=0 => No commit; no_changes_behavior=1 => Commit anyway; no_changes_behavior=2 => Exceptio
 0659        self.assert_is_git_repository(directory)
 0660        author_name = GeneralUtilities.str_none_safe(author_name).strip()
 0661        author_email = GeneralUtilities.str_none_safe(author_email).strip()
 0662        argument = ['commit', '--quiet', '--allow-empty', '--message', message]
 0663        if commit_message_body is not None:
 0664            argument.extend(['--message', commit_message_body])
 0665        if (GeneralUtilities.string_has_content(author_name)):
 0666            argument.append(f'--author="{author_name} <{author_email}>"')
 0667        git_repository_has_uncommitted_changes = self.git_repository_has_uncommitted_changes(directory)
 668
 0669        if git_repository_has_uncommitted_changes:
 0670            do_commit = True
 0671            if stage_all_changes:
 0672                self.git_stage_all_changes(directory)
 673        else:
 0674            if no_changes_behavior == 0:
 0675                self.log.log(f"Commit '{message}' will not be done because there are no changes to commit in repository 
 0676                do_commit = False
 0677            elif no_changes_behavior == 1:
 0678                self.log.log(f"There are no changes to commit in repository '{directory}'. Commit '{message}' will be do
 0679                do_commit = True
 0680            elif no_changes_behavior == 2:
 0681                raise RuntimeError(f"There are no changes to commit in repository '{directory}'. Commit '{message}' will
 682            else:
 0683                raise ValueError(f"Unknown value for no_changes_behavior: {GeneralUtilities.str_none_safe(no_changes_beh
 684
 0685        if do_commit:
 0686            self.log.log(f"Commit changes in '{directory}'", LogLevel.Information)
 0687            self.run_program_argsasarray("git", argument, directory, throw_exception_if_exitcode_is_not_zero=True)
 688
 0689        return self.git_get_commit_id(directory)
 690
 1691    def search_repository_folder(self,some_file_in_repository:str)->str:
 0692        current_path:str=os.path.dirname(some_file_in_repository)
 0693        enabled:bool=True
 0694        while enabled:
 0695            try:
 0696                current_path=GeneralUtilities.resolve_relative_path("..",current_path)
 0697                if self.is_git_repository(current_path):
 0698                    return current_path
 0699            except:
 0700                enabled=False
 0701        raise ValueError(f"Can not find git-repository for folder \"{some_file_in_repository}\".")
 702
 703

 1719    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments

 0724        self.run_program_argsasarray("git", ["checkout", rev], directory, throw_exception_if_exitcode_is_not_zero=True)

 0726        commit_id=self.git_get_commit_id(directory,"HEAD")

 1731    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 1732    def merge_repository(self, repository_folder: str, remote: str, branch: str):
 0733        GeneralUtilities.assert_condition(not self.git_repository_has_uncommitted_changes(repository_folder),f"Can not m
 0734        is_pullable: bool = self.git_commit_is_ancestor(repository_folder, branch, f"{remote}/{branch}")
 0735        if is_pullable:
 0736            self.git_pull(repository_folder, remote, branch, branch)
 0737            uncommitted_changes = self.git_repository_has_uncommitted_changes(repository_folder)
 0738            GeneralUtilities.assert_condition(not uncommitted_changes, f"Pulling remote \"{remote}\" in \"{repository_fo

 0740        self.git_fetch(repository_folder, remote)
 0741        self.git_merge(repository_folder, f"{remote}/{branch}", branch)
 0742        self.git_push_with_retry(repository_folder, remote, branch, branch)
 0743        self.git_checkout(repository_folder, branch)

 745

 1751    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments

 0757            args.append("--no-commit")

 0759            args.append("--no-ff")

 0761            args.append("-m")

 0763        args.append(sourcebranch)

 0765        self.run_program_argsasarray("git", ["submodule", "update"], directory, throw_exception_if_exitcode_is_not_zero=
 0766        return self.git_get_commit_id(directory)

 1775    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments

 0778            foldername = os.path.basename(subfolder)

 0780                source_repository = subfolder

 0782                if os.path.isdir(target_directory):
 783                    # fetch
 0784                    self.git_fetch(target_directory)
 785                else:
 786                    # clone
 0787                    self.git_clone(target_repository, source_repository, include_submodules=True, mirror=True)

 1833    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments

 0840            counter = counter+1

 0847                search_commit_result = self.run_program_argsasarray("git", ["log", f'--after="{date_as_string}"', f'--be
 0848                if search_commit_result[0] != 0 or not GeneralUtilities.string_has_nonwhitespace_content(search_commit_r
 0849                    raise ValueError(f"Can not calculate corresponding commit for tag '{tag}'.")
 0850                commit_id_new = search_commit_result[1]
 0851                self.git_delete_tag(repository, tag)
 0852                self.git_create_tag(repository, commit_id_new, tag, sign, message)

 1888    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 1889    def export_filemetadata(self, folder: str, target_file: str, encoding: str = "utf-8", filter_function=None) -> None:
 1890        folder = GeneralUtilities.resolve_relative_path_from_current_working_directory(folder)
 1891        lines = list()
 1892        path_prefix = len(folder)+1
 1893        items = dict()
 1894        for item in GeneralUtilities.get_all_folders_of_folder(folder):
 1895            items[item] = "d"
 1896        for item in GeneralUtilities.get_all_files_of_folder(folder):
 1897            items[item] = "f"
 1898        for file_or_folder, item_type in items.items():
 1899            truncated_file = file_or_folder[path_prefix:]
 1900            if (filter_function is None or filter_function(folder, truncated_file)):
 1901                owner_and_permisssion = self.get_file_owner_and_file_permission(file_or_folder)
 1902                user = owner_and_permisssion[0]
 1903                permissions = owner_and_permisssion[1]
 1904                lines.append(f"{truncated_file};{item_type};{user};{permissions}")
 1905        lines = sorted(lines, key=str.casefold)
 1906        with open(target_file, "w", encoding=encoding) as file_object:
 1907            file_object.write("\n".join(lines))
 908
 1909    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 1910    def escape_git_repositories_in_folder(self, folder: str) -> dict[str, str]:
 1911        return self.__escape_git_repositories_in_folder_internal(folder, dict[str, str]())
 912
 1913    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 1914    def __escape_git_repositories_in_folder_internal(self, folder: str, renamed_items: dict[str, str]) -> dict[str, str]
 1915        for file in GeneralUtilities.get_direct_files_of_folder(folder):
 1916            filename = os.path.basename(file)
 1917            if ".git" in filename:
 1918                new_name = filename.replace(".git", ".gitx")
 1919                target = os.path.join(folder, new_name)
 1920                os.rename(file, target)
 1921                renamed_items[target] = file
 1922        for subfolder in GeneralUtilities.get_direct_folders_of_folder(folder):
 1923            foldername = os.path.basename(subfolder)
 1924            if ".git" in foldername:
 1925                new_name = foldername.replace(".git", ".gitx")
 1926                subfolder2 = os.path.join(str(Path(subfolder).parent), new_name)
 1927                os.rename(subfolder, subfolder2)
 1928                renamed_items[subfolder2] = subfolder
 929            else:
 1930                subfolder2 = subfolder
 1931            self.__escape_git_repositories_in_folder_internal(subfolder2, renamed_items)
 1932        return renamed_items
 933
 1934    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 1935    def deescape_git_repositories_in_folder(self, renamed_items: dict[str, str]):
 1936        for renamed_item, original_name in renamed_items.items():
 1937            os.rename(renamed_item, original_name)
 938
 1939    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 1940    def is_git_repository(self, folder: str) -> bool:
 941        """This function works platform-independent also for non-local-executions if the ScriptCollection commandline-co
 1942        folder=folder.replace("\\","/")
 1943        if folder.endswith("/"):
 0944            folder = folder[:-1]
 1945        if not self.is_folder(folder):
 0946            raise ValueError(f"Folder '{folder}' does not exist.")
 1947        git_folder_path = f"{folder}/.git"
 1948        return self.is_folder(git_folder_path) or self.is_file(git_folder_path)
 949
 1950    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 1951    def is_bare_git_repository(self, folder: str) -> bool:
 952        """This function works platform-independent also for non-local-executions if the ScriptCollection commandline-co
 0953        if folder.endswith("/") or folder.endswith("\\"):
 0954            folder = folder[:-1]
 0955        if not self.is_folder(folder):
 0956            raise ValueError(f"Folder '{folder}' does not exist.")
 0957        return folder.endswith(".git")
 958
 1959    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 1960    def is_git_or_bare_git_repository(self, folder: str) -> bool:
 961        """This function works platform-independent also for non-local-executions if the ScriptCollection commandline-co
 1962        return self.is_git_repository(folder) or self.is_bare_git_repository(folder)
 963
 1964    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments

 0967        GeneralUtilities.assert_condition(self.is_git_repository(folder), f"'{folder}' is not a git-repository.")
 968
 1969    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 1970    def convert_git_repository_to_bare_repository(self, repository_folder: str):
 0971        repository_folder = repository_folder.replace("\\", "/")
 0972        self.assert_is_git_repository(repository_folder)
 0973        git_folder = repository_folder + "/.git"
 0974        if not self.is_folder(git_folder):
 0975            raise ValueError(f"Converting '{repository_folder}' to a bare repository not possible. The folder '{git_fold
 0976        target_folder: str = repository_folder + ".git"
 0977        GeneralUtilities.ensure_directory_exists(target_folder)
 0978        GeneralUtilities.move_content_of_folder(git_folder, target_folder)
 0979        GeneralUtilities.ensure_directory_does_not_exist(repository_folder)
 0980        self.run_program_argsasarray("git", ["config", "--bool", "core.bare", "true"], target_folder)
 981
 1982    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 1983    def assert_no_uncommitted_changes(self, repository_folder: str,custom_message:str=None) -> None:
 0984        if self.git_repository_has_uncommitted_changes(repository_folder):
 0985            if custom_message:
 0986                raise ValueError(f"Repository '{repository_folder}' has uncommitted changes: "+custom_message)
 987            else:
 0988                raise ValueError(f"Repository '{repository_folder}' has uncommitted changes.")
 989
 1990    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 1991    def list_content(self, path: str, include_files: bool, include_folder: bool, printonlynamewithoutpath: bool) -> list
 992        """This function works platform-independent also for non-local-executions if the ScriptCollection commandline-co
 0993        result: list[str] = []
 0994        if self.program_runner.will_be_executed_locally():
 0995            if include_files:
 0996                result = result + GeneralUtilities.get_direct_files_of_folder(path)
 0997            if include_folder:
 0998                result = result + GeneralUtilities.get_direct_folders_of_folder(path)
 999        else:
 01000            arguments = ["--path", path]
 01001            if not include_files:
 01002                arguments = arguments+["--excludefiles"]
 01003            if not include_folder:
 01004                arguments = arguments+["--excludedirectories"]
 01005            if printonlynamewithoutpath:
 01006                arguments = arguments+["--printonlynamewithoutpath"]
 01007            exit_code, stdout, stderr, _ = self.run_program_argsasarray("sclistfoldercontent", arguments)
 01008            if exit_code == 0:
 01009                for line in stdout.split("\n"):
 01010                    normalized_line = line.replace("\r", "")
 01011                    result.append(normalized_line)
 1012            else:
 01013                raise ValueError(f"Fatal error occurrs while checking whether file '{path}' exists. StdErr: '{stderr}'")
 01014        result = [item for item in result if GeneralUtilities.string_has_nonwhitespace_content(item)]
 01015        return result
 1016
 11017    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11018    def is_file(self, path: str) -> bool:
 1019        """This function works platform-independent also for non-local-executions if the ScriptCollection commandline-co
 11020        if self.program_runner.will_be_executed_locally():
 11021            return os.path.isfile(path)  # works only locally, but much more performant than always running an external 
 1022        else:
 01023            exit_code, _, stderr, _ = self.run_program_argsasarray("scfileexists", ["--path", path], throw_exception_if_
 01024            if exit_code == 0:
 01025                return True
 01026            elif exit_code == 1:
 01027                raise ValueError(f"Not calculatable whether file '{path}' exists. StdErr: '{stderr}'")
 01028            elif exit_code == 2:
 01029                return False
 01030            raise ValueError(f"Fatal error occurrs while checking whether file '{path}' exists. StdErr: '{stderr}'")
 1031

 1037        else:

 11044    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11045    def is_folder(self, path: str) -> bool:

 11060    def get_file_content(self, path: str, encoding: str = "utf-8") -> str:

 11193    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11194    def __sort_fmd(self, line: str):
 01195        splitted: list = line.split(";")
 01196        filetype: str = splitted[1]
 01197        if filetype == "d":
 01198            return -1
 01199        if filetype == "f":
 01200            return 1
 01201        return 0
 1202
 11203    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11204    def restore_filemetadata(self, folder: str, source_file: str, strict=False, encoding: str = "utf-8", create_folder_i
 01205        lines = GeneralUtilities.read_lines_from_file(source_file, encoding)
 01206        lines.sort(key=self.__sort_fmd)
 01207        for line in lines:
 01208            splitted: list = line.split(";")
 01209            full_path_of_file_or_folder: str = os.path.join(folder, splitted[0])
 01210            filetype: str = splitted[1]
 01211            user: str = splitted[2]
 01212            permissions: str = splitted[3]
 01213            if filetype == "d" and create_folder_is_not_exist and not os.path.isdir(full_path_of_file_or_folder):
 01214                GeneralUtilities.ensure_directory_exists(full_path_of_file_or_folder)
 01215            if (filetype == "f" and os.path.isfile(full_path_of_file_or_folder)) or (filetype == "d" and os.path.isdir(f
 01216                self.set_owner(full_path_of_file_or_folder, user, os.name != 'nt')
 01217                self.set_permission(full_path_of_file_or_folder, permissions)
 1218            else:
 01219                if strict:
 01220                    if filetype == "f":
 01221                        filetype_full = "File"
 01222                    elif filetype == "d":
 01223                        filetype_full = "Directory"
 1224                    else:
 01225                        raise ValueError(f"Unknown filetype: {GeneralUtilities.str_none_safe(filetype)}")
 01226                    raise ValueError(f"{filetype_full} '{full_path_of_file_or_folder}' does not exist")
 1227
 11228    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11229    def __calculate_lengh_in_seconds(self, filename: str, folder: str) -> float:
 01230        argument = ['-v', 'error', '-show_entries', 'format=duration', '-of', 'default=noprint_wrappers=1:nokey=1', file
 01231        result = self.run_program_argsasarray("ffprobe", argument, folder, throw_exception_if_exitcode_is_not_zero=True)
 01232        return float(result[1].replace('\n', ''))
 1233
 11234    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11235    def __create_thumbnails(self, filename: str, fps: str, folder: str, tempname_for_thumbnails: str) -> list[str]:
 01236        argument = ['-i', filename, '-r', fps, '-vf', 'scale=-1:120', '-vcodec', 'png', f'{tempname_for_thumbnails}-%002
 01237        self.run_program_argsasarray("ffmpeg", argument, folder, throw_exception_if_exitcode_is_not_zero=True)
 01238        files = GeneralUtilities.get_direct_files_of_folder(folder)
 01239        result: list[str] = []
 01240        regex = "^"+re.escape(tempname_for_thumbnails)+"\\-\\d+\\.png$"
 01241        regex_for_files = re.compile(regex)
 01242        for file in files:
 01243            filename = os.path.basename(file)
 01244            if regex_for_files.match(filename):
 01245                result.append(file)
 01246        GeneralUtilities.assert_condition(0 < len(result), "No thumbnail-files found.")
 01247        return result
 1248
 11249    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11250    def __create_thumbnail(self, outputfilename: str, folder: str, length_in_seconds: float, tempname_for_thumbnails: st
 01251        duration = timedelta(seconds=length_in_seconds)

 01253        next_square_number = GeneralUtilities.get_next_square_number(amount_of_images)
 01254        root = math.sqrt(next_square_number)
 01255        rows: int = root  # 5
 01256        columns: int = root  # math.ceil(amount_of_images/rows)
 01257        argument = ['-title', f'"{outputfilename} ({info})"', '-tile', f'{rows}x{columns}', f'{tempname_for_thumbnails}*
 01258        self.run_program_argsasarray("montage", argument, folder, throw_exception_if_exitcode_is_not_zero=True)
 1259

 11267    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11268    def __roundup(self, x: float, places: int) -> int:
 01269        d = 10 ** places
 01270        if x < 0:
 01271            return math.floor(x * d) / d
 1272        else:
 01273            return math.ceil(x * d) / d

 11275    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11276    def generate_thumbnail(self, file: str, frames_per_second: float, tempname_for_thumbnails: str = None, hook=None) ->
 01277        if tempname_for_thumbnails is None:
 01278            tempname_for_thumbnails = "t_"+str(uuid.uuid4())
 1279
 01280        file = GeneralUtilities.resolve_relative_path_from_current_working_directory(file)
 01281        filename = os.path.basename(file)
 01282        folder = os.path.dirname(file)
 01283        filename_without_extension = Path(file).stem
 01284        preview_files: list[str] = []
 01285        try:
 01286            length_in_seconds = self.__calculate_lengh_in_seconds(filename, folder)
 1287            # frames per second, example: frames_per_second="20fps" => 20 frames per second
 01288            frames_per_second = self.__roundup(float(frames_per_second[:-3]), 2)
 01289            frames_per_second_as_string = str(frames_per_second)
 01290            preview_files = self.__create_thumbnails(filename, frames_per_second_as_string, folder, tempname_for_thumbna
 01291            if hook is not None:
 01292                hook(file, preview_files)
 01293            actual_amounf_of_previewframes = len(preview_files)
 01294            self.__create_thumbnail(filename_without_extension, folder, length_in_seconds, tempname_for_thumbnails, actu
 1295        finally:
 01296            for thumbnail_to_delete in preview_files:
 01297                os.remove(thumbnail_to_delete)

 11299    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11300    def generate_thumbnail_by_amount_of_pictures(self, file: str, amount_of_columns: int, amount_of_rows: int, tempname_
 01301        if tempname_for_thumbnails is None:
 01302            tempname_for_thumbnails = "t_"+str(uuid.uuid4())
 1303
 01304        file = GeneralUtilities.resolve_relative_path_from_current_working_directory(file)
 01305        filename = os.path.basename(file)
 01306        folder = os.path.dirname(file)
 01307        filename_without_extension = Path(file).stem
 01308        preview_files: list[str] = []
 01309        try:
 01310            length_in_seconds = self.__calculate_lengh_in_seconds(filename, folder)
 01311            amounf_of_previewframes = int(amount_of_columns*amount_of_rows)
 01312            frames_per_second_as_string = f"{amounf_of_previewframes-2}/{length_in_seconds}"
 01313            preview_files = self.__create_thumbnails(filename, frames_per_second_as_string, folder, tempname_for_thumbna
 01314            if hook is not None:
 01315                hook(file, preview_files)
 01316            actual_amounf_of_previewframes = len(preview_files)
 01317            self.__create_thumbnail2(filename_without_extension, folder, length_in_seconds, amount_of_rows, amount_of_co
 1318        finally:
 01319            for thumbnail_to_delete in preview_files:
 01320                os.remove(thumbnail_to_delete)

 11322    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11323    def extract_pdf_pages(self, file: str, from_page: int, to_page: int, outputfile: str) -> None:
 01324        pdf_reader: PdfReader = PdfReader(file)
 01325        pdf_writer: PdfWriter = PdfWriter()
 01326        start = from_page
 01327        end = to_page
 01328        while start <= end:
 01329            pdf_writer.add_page(pdf_reader.pages[start-1])
 01330            start += 1
 01331        with open(outputfile, 'wb') as out:
 01332            pdf_writer.write(out)
 1333
 11334    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11335    def merge_pdf_files(self, files: list[str], outputfile: str) -> None:
 1336        # TODO add wildcard-option
 01337        pdfFileMerger: PdfWriter = PdfWriter()
 01338        for file in files:
 01339            with open(file, "rb") as f:
 01340                pdfFileMerger.append(f)
 01341        with open(outputfile, "wb") as output:
 01342            pdfFileMerger.write(output)
 01343            pdfFileMerger.close()
 1344

 11350    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11351    def SCCreateEmptyFileWithSpecificSize(self, name: str, size_string: str) -> int:
 01352        if size_string.isdigit():
 01353            size = int(size_string)
 1354        else:
 01355            if len(size_string) >= 3:
 01356                if (size_string.endswith("kb")):
 01357                    size = int(size_string[:-2]) * pow(10, 3)
 01358                elif (size_string.endswith("mb")):
 01359                    size = int(size_string[:-2]) * pow(10, 6)
 01360                elif (size_string.endswith("gb")):
 01361                    size = int(size_string[:-2]) * pow(10, 9)
 01362                elif (size_string.endswith("kib")):
 01363                    size = int(size_string[:-3]) * pow(2, 10)
 01364                elif (size_string.endswith("mib")):
 01365                    size = int(size_string[:-3]) * pow(2, 20)
 01366                elif (size_string.endswith("gib")):
 01367                    size = int(size_string[:-3]) * pow(2, 30)
 1368                else:
 01369                    self.log.log("Wrong format", LogLevel.Error)
 01370                    return 1
 1371            else:
 01372                self.log.log("Wrong format", LogLevel.Error)
 01373                return 1
 01374        with open(name, "wb") as f:
 01375            f.seek(size-1)
 01376            f.write(b"\0")
 01377        return 0
 1378
 11379    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11380    def SCCreateHashOfAllFiles(self, folder: str) -> None:
 01381        for file in GeneralUtilities.absolute_file_paths(folder):

 11385    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11386    def SCCreateSimpleMergeWithoutRelease(self, repository: str, sourcebranch: str, targetbranch: str, remotename: str, 
 01387        commitid = self.git_merge(repository, sourcebranch, targetbranch, False, True)
 01388        self.git_merge(repository, targetbranch, sourcebranch, True, True)
 01389        created_version = self.get_semver_version_from_gitversion(repository)

 01391        self.git_push(repository, remotename, targetbranch, targetbranch, False, True)
 01392        if (GeneralUtilities.string_has_nonwhitespace_content(remotename)):
 01393            self.git_push(repository, remotename, sourcebranch, sourcebranch, False, True)
 01394        if (remove_source_branch):
 01395            self.git_remove_branch(repository, sourcebranch)

 11397    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11398    def sc_organize_lines_in_file(self, file: str, encoding: str="utf-8", sort: bool = False, remove_duplicated_lines: b
 11399        GeneralUtilities.assert_file_exists(file)
 1400
 1401        # read file
 11402        lines = GeneralUtilities.read_lines_from_file(file, encoding)
 11403        if (len(lines) == 0):
 01404            return
 1405
 1406        # store first line if desired
 11407        if (ignore_first_line):
 11408            first_line = lines.pop(0)
 1409
 1410        # remove empty lines if desired
 11411        if remove_empty_lines:
 11412            temp = lines
 11413            lines = []
 11414            for line in temp:
 11415                if (not (GeneralUtilities.string_is_none_or_whitespace(line))):
 11416                    lines.append(line)
 1417
 1418        # remove duplicated lines if desired
 11419        if remove_duplicated_lines:
 11420            lines = GeneralUtilities.remove_duplicates(lines)
 1421
 1422        # sort lines if desired
 11423        if sort:
 11424            lines = sorted(lines, key=lambda singleline: self.__adapt_line_for_sorting(singleline, ignored_start_charact
 1425
 1426        # reinsert first line if required
 11427        if ignore_first_line:
 11428            lines.insert(0, first_line)
 1429
 1430        # write result to file
 11431        GeneralUtilities.write_lines_to_file(file, lines, encoding)
 1432
 1433
 11434    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11435    def __adapt_line_for_sorting(self, line: str, ignored_start_characters: list):
 11436        result = line.lower()
 11437        while len(result) > 0 and result[0] in ignored_start_characters:
 11438            result = result[1:]
 11439        return result
 1440
 11441    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11442    def SCGenerateSnkFiles(self, outputfolder, keysize=4096, amountofkeys=10) -> int:
 01443        GeneralUtilities.ensure_directory_exists(outputfolder)
 01444        for _ in range(amountofkeys):
 01445            file = os.path.join(outputfolder, str(uuid.uuid4())+".snk")
 01446            argument = f"-k {keysize} {file}"
 01447            self.run_program("sn", argument, outputfolder)
 1448
 11449    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11450    def __merge_files(self, sourcefile: str, targetfile: str) -> None:
 01451        with open(sourcefile, "rb") as f:
 01452            source_data = f.read()
 01453        with open(targetfile, "ab") as fout:
 01454            merge_separator = [0x0A]
 01455            fout.write(bytes(merge_separator))
 01456            fout.write(source_data)
 1457
 11458    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11459    def __process_file(self, file: str, substringInFilename: str, newSubstringInFilename: str, conflictResolveMode: str)
 01460        new_filename = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(file), os.path.basename(file).replace(substringInFilename, newSubstr
 01461        if file != new_filename:
 01462            if os.path.isfile(new_filename):
 01463                if filecmp.cmp(file, new_filename):
 01464                    send2trash.send2trash(file)
 1465                else:
 01466                    if conflictResolveMode == "ignore":
 01467                        pass
 01468                    elif conflictResolveMode == "preservenewest":
 01469                        if (os.path.getmtime(file) - os.path.getmtime(new_filename) > 0):
 01470                            send2trash.send2trash(file)
 1471                        else:
 01472                            send2trash.send2trash(new_filename)
 01473                            os.rename(file, new_filename)
 01474                    elif (conflictResolveMode == "merge"):
 01475                        self.__merge_files(file, new_filename)
 01476                        send2trash.send2trash(file)
 1477                    else:
 01478                        raise ValueError('Unknown conflict resolve mode')
 1479            else:
 01480                os.rename(file, new_filename)
 1481
 11482    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11483    def SCReplaceSubstringsInFilenames(self, folder: str, substringInFilename: str, newSubstringInFilename: str, conflic
 01484        for file in GeneralUtilities.absolute_file_paths(folder):
 01485            self.__process_file(file, substringInFilename, newSubstringInFilename, conflictResolveMode)
 1486
 11487    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11488    def __check_file(self, file: str, searchstring: str) -> None:
 01489        bytes_ascii = bytes(searchstring, "ascii")
 1490        # often called "unicode-encoding"
 01491        bytes_utf16 = bytes(searchstring, "utf-16")
 01492        bytes_utf8 = bytes(searchstring, "utf-8")
 01493        with open(file, mode='rb') as file_object:
 01494            content = file_object.read()
 01495            if bytes_ascii in content:
 01496                GeneralUtilities.write_message_to_stdout(file)
 01497            elif bytes_utf16 in content:
 01498                GeneralUtilities.write_message_to_stdout(file)
 01499            elif bytes_utf8 in content:
 01500                GeneralUtilities.write_message_to_stdout(file)
 1501
 11502    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11503    def SCSearchInFiles(self, folder: str, searchstring: str) -> None:
 01504        for file in GeneralUtilities.absolute_file_paths(folder):
 01505            self.__check_file(file, searchstring)

 01511        f = io.StringIO()
 01512        qr.print_ascii(out=f)
 01513        f.seek(0)
 01514        return f.read()

 11516    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11517    def __print_qr_code_by_csv_line(self, displayname: str, website: str, emailaddress: str, key: str, period: str) -> N
 01518        qrcode_content = f"otpauth://totp/{website}:{emailaddress}?secret={key}&issuer={displayname}&period={period}"
 01519        GeneralUtilities.write_message_to_stdout(f"{displayname} ({emailaddress}):")
 01520        GeneralUtilities.write_message_to_stdout(qrcode_content)
 01521        GeneralUtilities.write_message_to_stdout(self.get_string_as_qr_code(qrcode_content))

 11523    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11524    def SCShow2FAAsQRCode(self, csvfile: str) -> None:

 01527        for line in lines:
 01528            self.__print_qr_code_by_csv_line(line[0], line[1], line[2], line[3], line[4])
 01529            GeneralUtilities.write_message_to_stdout(GeneralUtilities.get_longline())
 1530
 11531    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11532    def SCCalculateBitcoinBlockHash(self, block_version_number: str, previousblockhash: str, transactionsmerkleroot: str
 1533        # Example-values:
 1534        # block_version_number: "00000020"
 1535        # previousblockhash: "66720b99e07d284bd4fe67ff8c49a5db1dd8514fcdab61000000000000000000"
 1536        # transactionsmerkleroot: "7829844f4c3a41a537b3131ca992643eaa9d093b2383e4cdc060ad7dc5481187"
 1537        # timestamp: "51eb505a"
 1538        # target: "c1910018"
 1539        # nonce: "de19b302"
 01540        header = str(block_version_number + previousblockhash + transactionsmerkleroot + timestamp + target + nonce)
 01541        return binascii.hexlify(hashlib.sha256(hashlib.sha256(binascii.unhexlify(header)).digest()).digest()[::-1]).deco
 1542
 11543    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11544    def SCChangeHashOfProgram(self, inputfile: str) -> None:
 01545        valuetoappend = str(uuid.uuid4())
 1546
 01547        outputfile = inputfile + '.modified'
 1548
 01549        shutil.copy2(inputfile, outputfile)
 01550        with open(outputfile, 'a', encoding="utf-8") as file:
 1551            # TODO use rcedit for .exe-files instead of appending valuetoappend ( https://github.com/electron/rcedit/ )
 1552            # background: you can retrieve the "original-filename" from the .exe-file like discussed here:
 1553            # https://security.stackexchange.com/questions/210843/ is-it-possible-to-change-original-filename-of-an-exe
 1554            # so removing the original filename with rcedit is probably a better way to make it more difficult to detect
 1555            # this would obviously also change the hashvalue of the program so appending a whitespace is not required an
 01556            file.write(valuetoappend)
 1557
 11558    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11559    def __adjust_folder_name(self, folder: str) -> str:
 01560        result = os.path.dirname(folder).replace("\\", "/")
 01561        if result == "/":
 01562            return GeneralUtilities.empty_string
 1563        else:
 01564            return result
 1565
 11566    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11567    def __create_iso(self, folder, iso_file) -> None:
 01568        created_directories = []
 01569        files_directory = "FILES"
 01570        iso = pycdlib.PyCdlib()
 01571        iso.new()
 01572        files_directory = files_directory.upper()
 01573        iso.add_directory("/" + files_directory)
 01574        created_directories.append("/" + files_directory)
 01575        for root, _, files in os.walk(folder):
 01576            for file in files:
 01577                full_path = os.path.join(root, file)
 01578                with (open(full_path, "rb").read()) as text_io_wrapper:
 01579                    content = text_io_wrapper
 01580                    path_in_iso = '/' + files_directory + \
 1581                        self.__adjust_folder_name(full_path[len(folder)::1]).upper()
 01582                    if path_in_iso not in created_directories:
 01583                        iso.add_directory(path_in_iso)
 01584                        created_directories.append(path_in_iso)
 01585                    iso.add_fp(BytesIO(content), len(content), path_in_iso + '/' + file.upper() + ';1')
 01586        iso.write(iso_file)
 01587        iso.close()
 1588
 11589    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11590    def SCCreateISOFileWithObfuscatedFiles(self, inputfolder: str, outputfile: str, printtableheadline, createisofile, e
 01591        if (os.path.isdir(inputfolder)):
 01592            namemappingfile = "name_map.csv"
 01593            files_directory = inputfolder
 01594            files_directory_obf = f"{files_directory}_Obfuscated"
 01595            self.SCObfuscateFilesFolder(
 1596                inputfolder, printtableheadline, namemappingfile, extensions)
 01597            os.rename(namemappingfile, os.path.join(
 1598                files_directory_obf, namemappingfile))
 01599            if createisofile:
 01600                self.__create_iso(files_directory_obf, outputfile)
 01601                shutil.rmtree(files_directory_obf)
 1602        else:
 01603            raise ValueError(f"Directory not found: '{inputfolder}'")
 1604
 11605    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11606    def SCFilenameObfuscator(self, inputfolder: str, printtableheadline, namemappingfile: str, extensions: str) -> None:
 01607        obfuscate_all_files = extensions == "*"
 01608        if (obfuscate_all_files):
 01609            obfuscate_file_extensions = None
 1610        else:
 01611            obfuscate_file_extensions = extensions.split(",")
 01612        if (os.path.isdir(inputfolder)):
 01613            printtableheadline = GeneralUtilities.string_to_boolean(
 1614                printtableheadline)
 01615            files = []
 01616            if not os.path.isfile(namemappingfile):
 01617                with open(namemappingfile, "a", encoding="utf-8"):
 01618                    pass
 01619            if printtableheadline:
 01620                GeneralUtilities.append_line_to_file(
 1621                    namemappingfile, "Original filename;new filename;SHA2-hash of file")
 01622            for file in GeneralUtilities.absolute_file_paths(inputfolder):
 01623                if os.path.isfile(os.path.join(inputfolder, file)):
 01624                    if obfuscate_all_files or self.__extension_matchs(file, obfuscate_file_extensions):
 01625                        files.append(file)
 01626            for file in files:
 01627                hash_value = GeneralUtilities.get_sha256_of_file(file)
 01628                extension = Path(file).suffix
 01629                new_file_name_without_path = str(uuid.uuid4())[0:8] + extension
 01630                new_file_name = os.path.join(
 1631                    os.path.dirname(file), new_file_name_without_path)
 01632                os.rename(file, new_file_name)
 01633                GeneralUtilities.append_line_to_file(namemappingfile, os.path.basename(file) + ";" + new_file_name_witho
 1634        else:
 01635            raise ValueError(f"Directory not found: '{inputfolder}'")
 1636
 11637    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11638    def __extension_matchs(self, file: str, obfuscate_file_extensions) -> bool:
 01639        for extension in obfuscate_file_extensions:
 01640            if file.lower().endswith("."+extension.lower()):
 01641                return True
 01642        return False
 1643
 11644    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11645    def SCHealthcheck(self, file: str) -> int:
 01646        lines = GeneralUtilities.read_lines_from_file(file)
 01647        for line in reversed(lines):
 01648            if not GeneralUtilities.string_is_none_or_whitespace(line):
 01649                if "RunningHealthy (" in line:  # TODO use regex
 01650                    GeneralUtilities.write_message_to_stderr(f"Healthy running due to line '{line}' in file '{file}'.")
 01651                    return 0
 1652                else:
 01653                    GeneralUtilities.write_message_to_stderr(f"Not healthy running due to line '{line}' in file '{file}'
 01654                    return 1
 01655        GeneralUtilities.write_message_to_stderr(f"No valid line found for healthycheck in file '{file}'.")
 01656        return 2
 1657
 11658    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11659    def SCObfuscateFilesFolder(self, inputfolder: str, printtableheadline, namemappingfile: str, extensions: str) -> Non
 01660        obfuscate_all_files = extensions == "*"
 01661        if (obfuscate_all_files):
 01662            obfuscate_file_extensions = None
 1663        else:
 01664            if "," in extensions:
 01665                obfuscate_file_extensions = extensions.split(",")
 1666            else:
 01667                obfuscate_file_extensions = [extensions]
 01668        newd = inputfolder+"_Obfuscated"
 01669        shutil.copytree(inputfolder, newd)
 01670        inputfolder = newd
 01671        if (os.path.isdir(inputfolder)):
 01672            for file in GeneralUtilities.absolute_file_paths(inputfolder):
 01673                if obfuscate_all_files or self.__extension_matchs(file, obfuscate_file_extensions):
 01674                    self.SCChangeHashOfProgram(file)
 01675                    os.remove(file)
 01676                    os.rename(file + ".modified", file)
 01677            self.SCFilenameObfuscator(inputfolder, printtableheadline, namemappingfile, extensions)
 1678        else:
 01679            raise ValueError(f"Directory not found: '{inputfolder}'")
 1680

 01686            result = list(services.keys())
 01687            return result

 11693    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11694    def get_latest_apt_package_version_in_debian(self, image: str,package:str) -> str:
 1695        #docker run --rm -it debian bash -c "apt update && apt list -a tor"
 01696        output=self.run_with_epew("docker", f"run --rm -it {image} bash -c \"apt --color=false update && apt --color=fal
 01697        stdout=output[1]
 01698        version_lines=[line.strip() for line in GeneralUtilities.string_to_lines(stdout) if GeneralUtilities.string_has_
 01699        GeneralUtilities.assert_condition(0<len(version_lines), f"No version found for package '{package}' in image '{im
 01700        versions = [version_line.split(" ")[1] for version_line in version_lines]
 01701        def my_comparer(a, b) -> int:
 1702            # return:
 1703            #  -1 → a < b
 1704            #   0 → a = b
 1705            #   1 → a > b
 1706            # dpkg --compare-versions <a> lt <b>  → exit 0 wenn a < b
 01707            def dpkg_compare(op: str) -> bool:
 01708                result = self.run_program_argsasarray("docker", [ "run", "--rm",image, "dpkg", "--compare-versions", a, 
 01709                GeneralUtilities.assert_condition(result[1]==GeneralUtilities.empty_string)
 01710                GeneralUtilities.assert_condition(result[2]==GeneralUtilities.empty_string)
 01711                return result[0] == 0
 1712
 01713            if dpkg_compare("lt"): # a < b
 01714                return -1
 01715            elif dpkg_compare("gt"): # a > b
 01716                return 1
 1717            else:
 01718                return 0
 01719        sorted_versions = sorted(versions, key=cmp_to_key(my_comparer))
 01720        return sorted_versions[-1]
 1721
 11722    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments

 01726        self.run_program("coverage", "xml", repository_folder)

 01728        coveragefile = os.path.join(repository_folder, "Other/TestCoverage/TestCoverage.xml")
 01729        GeneralUtilities.ensure_file_does_not_exist(coveragefile)
 01730        os.rename(os.path.join(repository_folder, "coverage.xml"), coveragefile)

 1763
 11764    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11765    def get_file_owner_and_file_permission(self, file: str) -> str:
 11766        ls_output: str = self.run_ls_for_folder(file)
 11767        return [self.__get_file_owner_helper(ls_output), self.__get_file_permission_helper(ls_output)]
 1768
 11769    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11770    def run_ls_for_folder(self, file_or_folder: str) -> str:
 11771        file_or_folder = file_or_folder.replace("\\", "/")
 11772        GeneralUtilities.assert_condition(os.path.isfile(file_or_folder) or os.path.isdir(file_or_folder), f"Can not exe
 11773        ls_result = self.run_program_argsasarray("ls", ["-ld", file_or_folder])
 11774        GeneralUtilities.assert_condition(ls_result[0] == 0, f"'ls -ld {file_or_folder}' resulted in exitcode {str(ls_re
 11775        GeneralUtilities.assert_condition(not GeneralUtilities.string_is_none_or_whitespace(ls_result[1]), f"'ls -ld' of
 11776        output = ls_result[1]
 11777        result = output.replace("\n", GeneralUtilities.empty_string)
 11778        result = ' '.join(result.split())   # reduce multiple whitespaces to one
 11779        return result
 1780
 11781    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11782    def run_ls_for_folder_content(self, file_or_folder: str) -> list[str]:
 11783        file_or_folder = file_or_folder.replace("\\", "/")
 11784        GeneralUtilities.assert_condition(os.path.isfile(file_or_folder) or os.path.isdir(file_or_folder), f"Can not exe
 11785        ls_result = self.run_program_argsasarray("ls", ["-la", file_or_folder])
 11786        GeneralUtilities.assert_condition(ls_result[0] == 0, f"'ls -la {file_or_folder}' resulted in exitcode {str(ls_re
 11787        GeneralUtilities.assert_condition(not GeneralUtilities.string_is_none_or_whitespace(ls_result[1]), f"'ls -la' of
 11788        output = ls_result[1]
 11789        result = output.split("\n")[3:]  # skip the lines with "Total", "." and ".."
 11790        result = [' '.join(line.split()) for line in result]  # reduce multiple whitespaces to one
 11791        return result
 1792
 11793    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11794    def set_permission(self, file_or_folder: str, permissions: str, recursive: bool = False) -> None:
 1795        """This function expects an usual octet-triple, for example "700"."""
 01796        args = []
 01797        if recursive:
 01798            args.append("--recursive")
 01799        args.append(permissions)
 01800        args.append(file_or_folder)
 01801        self.run_program_argsasarray("chmod", args)
 1802
 11803    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11804    def set_owner(self, file_or_folder: str, owner: str, recursive: bool = False, follow_symlinks: bool = False) -> None
 1805        """This function expects the user and the group in the format "user:group"."""
 01806        args = []
 01807        if recursive:
 01808            args.append("--recursive")
 01809        if follow_symlinks:
 01810            args.append("--no-dereference")
 01811        args.append(owner)
 01812        args.append(file_or_folder)
 01813        self.run_program_argsasarray("chown", args)
 1814
 1815    # <run programs>
 1816
 11817    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11818    def __run_program_argsasarray_async_helper(self, program: str, arguments_as_array: list[str] = [], working_directory
 11819        popen: Popen = self.program_runner.run_program_argsasarray_async_helper(program, arguments_as_array, working_dir
 11820        return popen
 1821
 11822    @staticmethod
 11823    def __enqueue_output(file: IO, queue: Queue):
 11824        for line in iter(file.readline, ''):
 11825            queue.put(line)
 11826        file.close()
 1827
 11828    @staticmethod
 11829    def __continue_process_reading(pid: int, p: Popen, q_stdout: Queue, q_stderr: Queue, reading_stdout_last_time_result
 11830        if p.poll() is None:
 11831            return True
 1832
 1833        # if reading_stdout_last_time_resulted_in_exception and reading_stderr_last_time_resulted_in_exception:
 1834        #    return False
 1835
 11836        if not q_stdout.empty():
 11837            return True
 1838
 11839        if not q_stderr.empty():
 01840            return True
 1841
 11842        return False
 1843
 11844    @staticmethod
 11845    def __read_popen_pipes(p: Popen, print_live_output: bool, print_errors_as_information: bool, log: SCLog) -> tuple[li
 11846        p_id = p.pid
 11847        with ThreadPoolExecutor(2) as pool:
 11848            q_stdout = Queue()
 11849            q_stderr = Queue()
 1850
 11851            pool.submit(ScriptCollectionCore.__enqueue_output, p.stdout, q_stdout)
 11852            pool.submit(ScriptCollectionCore.__enqueue_output, p.stderr, q_stderr)
 11853            reading_stdout_last_time_resulted_in_exception: bool = False
 11854            reading_stderr_last_time_resulted_in_exception: bool = False
 1855
 11856            stdout_result: list[str] = []
 11857            stderr_result: list[str] = []
 1858
 11859            while (ScriptCollectionCore.__continue_process_reading(p_id, p, q_stdout, q_stderr, reading_stdout_last_time
 11860                try:
 11861                    while not q_stdout.empty():
 11862                        out_line: str = q_stdout.get_nowait()
 11863                        out_line = out_line.replace("\r", GeneralUtilities.empty_string).replace("\n", GeneralUtilities.
 11864                        if GeneralUtilities.string_has_content(out_line):
 11865                            stdout_result.append(out_line)
 11866                            reading_stdout_last_time_resulted_in_exception = False
 11867                            if print_live_output:
 01868                                loglevel = LogLevel.Information
 01869                                if out_line.startswith("Debug: "):
 01870                                    loglevel = LogLevel.Debug
 01871                                    out_line = out_line[len("Debug: "):]
 01872                                if out_line.startswith("Diagnostic: "):
 01873                                    loglevel = LogLevel.Diagnostic
 01874                                    out_line = out_line[len("Diagnostic: "):]
 01875                                log.log(out_line, loglevel)
 01876                except Empty:
 01877                    reading_stdout_last_time_resulted_in_exception = True
 1878
 11879                try:
 11880                    while not q_stderr.empty():
 01881                        err_line: str = q_stderr.get_nowait()
 01882                        err_line = err_line.replace("\r", GeneralUtilities.empty_string).replace("\n", GeneralUtilities.
 01883                        if GeneralUtilities.string_has_content(err_line):
 01884                            stderr_result.append(err_line)
 01885                            reading_stderr_last_time_resulted_in_exception = False
 01886                            if print_live_output:
 01887                                loglevel = LogLevel.Error
 01888                                if err_line.startswith("Warning: "):
 01889                                    loglevel = LogLevel.Warning
 01890                                    err_line = err_line[len("Warning: "):]
 01891                                if print_errors_as_information:  # "errors" in "print_errors_as_information" means: all 
 01892                                    loglevel = LogLevel.Information
 01893                                log.log(err_line, loglevel)
 01894                except Empty:
 01895                    reading_stderr_last_time_resulted_in_exception = True
 1896
 11897                time.sleep(0.01)  # this is required to not finish too early
 1898
 11899            return (stdout_result, stderr_result)
 1900
 11901    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 11902    def run_program_argsasarray(self, program: str, arguments_as_array: list[str] = [], working_directory: str = None, p
 11903        if self.call_program_runner_directly:
 01904            return self.program_runner.run_program_argsasarray(program, arguments_as_array, working_directory, custom_ar
 11905        try:
 11906            GeneralUtilities.assert_not_null(arguments_as_array,"arguments_as_array must not be null")
 11907            arguments_as_str = ' '.join(arguments_as_array)
 11908            mock_loader_result = self.__try_load_mock(program, arguments_as_str, working_directory)
 11909            if mock_loader_result[0]:
 11910                return mock_loader_result[1]
 1911
 11912            if self.program_runner.will_be_executed_locally():
 11913                working_directory = self.__adapt_workingdirectory(working_directory)
 1914
 11915            if arguments_for_log is None or len(arguments_for_log)==0:
 11916                arguments_for_log = arguments_as_array
 1917
 11918            cmd = f'{GeneralUtilities.str_none_safe(working_directory)}>{program}'
 11919            if 0 < len(arguments_for_log):
 11920                arguments_for_log_as_string: str = ' '.join([f'"{argument_for_log}"' for argument_for_log in arguments_f
 11921                cmd = f'{cmd} {arguments_for_log_as_string}'
 1922
 11923            if GeneralUtilities.string_is_none_or_whitespace(title):
 11924                info_for_log = cmd
 1925            else:
 01926                info_for_log = title
 1927
 11928            self.log.log(f"Run '{info_for_log}'.", LogLevel.Debug)
 1929
 11930            exit_code: int = None
 11931            stdout: str = GeneralUtilities.empty_string
 11932            stderr: str = GeneralUtilities.empty_string
 11933            pid: int = None
 1934
 11935            with self.__run_program_argsasarray_async_helper(program, arguments_as_array, working_directory,  print_erro
 1936
 11937                if log_file is not None:
 01938                    GeneralUtilities.ensure_file_exists(log_file)
 11939                pid = process.pid
 1940
 11941                outputs: tuple[list[str], list[str]] = ScriptCollectionCore.__read_popen_pipes(process, print_live_outpu
 1942
 11943                for out_line_plain in outputs[0]:
 11944                    if out_line_plain is not None:
 11945                        out_line: str = None
 11946                        if isinstance(out_line_plain, str):
 11947                            out_line = out_line_plain
 01948                        elif isinstance(out_line_plain, bytes):
 01949                            out_line = GeneralUtilities.bytes_to_string(out_line_plain)
 1950                        else:
 01951                            raise ValueError(f"Unknown type of output: {str(type(out_line_plain))}")
 1952
 11953                        if out_line is not None and GeneralUtilities.string_has_content(out_line):
 11954                            if out_line.endswith("\n"):
 01955                                out_line = out_line[:-1]
 11956                            if 0 < len(stdout):
 11957                                stdout = stdout+"\n"
 11958                            stdout = stdout+out_line
 11959                            if log_file is not None:
 01960                                GeneralUtilities.append_line_to_file(log_file, out_line)
 1961
 11962                for err_line_plain in outputs[1]:
 01963                    if err_line_plain is not None:
 01964                        err_line: str = None
 01965                        if isinstance(err_line_plain, str):
 01966                            err_line = err_line_plain
 01967                        elif isinstance(err_line_plain, bytes):
 01968                            err_line = GeneralUtilities.bytes_to_string(err_line_plain)
 1969                        else:
 01970                            raise ValueError(f"Unknown type of output: {str(type(err_line_plain))}")
 01971                        if err_line is not None and GeneralUtilities.string_has_content(err_line):
 01972                            if err_line.endswith("\n"):
 01973                                err_line = err_line[:-1]
 01974                            if 0 < len(stderr):
 01975                                stderr = stderr+"\n"
 01976                            stderr = stderr+err_line
 01977                            if log_file is not None:
 01978                                GeneralUtilities.append_line_to_file(log_file, err_line)
 1979
 11980            exit_code = process.returncode
 11981            GeneralUtilities.assert_condition(exit_code is not None, f"Exitcode of program-run of '{info_for_log}' is No
 1982
 11983            result_message = f"Program '{info_for_log}' resulted in exitcode {exit_code}."
 1984
 11985            self.log.log(result_message, LogLevel.Debug)
 1986
 11987            if throw_exception_if_exitcode_is_not_zero and exit_code != 0:
 01988                raise ValueError(f"{result_message} (StdOut: '{stdout}', StdErr: '{stderr}')")
 1989
 11990            result = (exit_code, stdout, stderr, pid)
 11991            return result
 01992        except Exception as e:#pylint:disable=unused-variable, try-except-raise
 01993            raise

 12031    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12032    def __try_load_mock(self, program: str, arguments: str, working_directory: str) -> tuple[bool, tuple[int, str, str, 
 12033        if self.mock_program_calls:
 12034            try:
 12035                return [True, self.__get_mock_program_call(program, arguments, working_directory)]
 02036            except LookupError:
 02037                if not self.execute_program_really_if_no_mock_call_is_defined:
 02038                    raise
 12039        return [False, None]
 2040
 12041    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12042    def __adapt_workingdirectory(self, workingdirectory: str) -> str:
 12043        result: str = None
 12044        if workingdirectory is None:
 12045            result = os.getcwd()
 2046        else:
 12047            if os.path.isabs(workingdirectory):
 12048                result = workingdirectory
 2049            else:
 02050                result = GeneralUtilities.resolve_relative_path_from_current_working_directory(workingdirectory)
 12051        if not os.path.isdir(result):
 02052            raise ValueError(f"Working-directory '{workingdirectory}' does not exist.")
 12053        return result
 2054
 12055    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12056    def verify_no_pending_mock_program_calls(self):
 12057        if (len(self.__mocked_program_calls) > 0):
 02058            raise AssertionError("The following mock-calls were not called:\n"+",\n    ".join([self.__format_mock_progra
 2059
 12060    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12061    def __format_mock_program_call(self, r) -> str:
 02062        r: ScriptCollectionCore.__MockProgramCall = r
 02063        return f"'{r.workingdirectory}>{r.program} {r.argument}' (" \
 2064            f"exitcode: {GeneralUtilities.str_none_safe(str(r.exit_code))}, " \
 2065            f"pid: {GeneralUtilities.str_none_safe(str(r.pid))}, "\
 2066            f"stdout: {GeneralUtilities.str_none_safe(str(r.stdout))}, " \
 2067            f"stderr: {GeneralUtilities.str_none_safe(str(r.stderr))})"
 2068
 12069    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12070    def register_mock_program_call(self, program: str, argument: str, workingdirectory: str, result_exit_code: int, resu
 2071        "This function is for test-purposes only"
 12072        for _ in itertools.repeat(None, amount_of_expected_calls):
 12073            mock_call = ScriptCollectionCore.__MockProgramCall()
 12074            mock_call.program = program
 12075            mock_call.argument = argument
 12076            mock_call.workingdirectory = workingdirectory
 12077            mock_call.exit_code = result_exit_code
 12078            mock_call.stdout = result_stdout
 12079            mock_call.stderr = result_stderr
 12080            mock_call.pid = result_pid
 12081            self.__mocked_program_calls.append(mock_call)
 2082
 12083    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12084    def __get_mock_program_call(self, program: str, argument: str, workingdirectory: str):
 12085        result: ScriptCollectionCore.__MockProgramCall = None
 12086        for mock_call in self.__mocked_program_calls:
 12087            if ((re.match(mock_call.program, program) is not None)
 2088               and (re.match(mock_call.argument, argument) is not None)
 2089               and (re.match(mock_call.workingdirectory, workingdirectory) is not None)):
 12090                result = mock_call
 12091                break
 12092        if result is None:
 02093            raise LookupError(f"Tried to execute mock-call '{workingdirectory}>{program} {argument}' but no mock-call wa
 2094        else:
 12095            self.__mocked_program_calls.remove(result)
 12096            return (result.exit_code, result.stdout, result.stderr, result.pid)
 2097
 12098    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12099    class __MockProgramCall:
 12100        program: str
 12101        argument: str
 12102        workingdirectory: str
 12103        exit_code: int
 12104        stdout: str
 12105        stderr: str
 12106        pid: int
 2107
 12108    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12109    def run_with_epew_with_retry(self, program: str, argument: str = "", working_directory: str = None, print_errors_as_
 02110        return GeneralUtilities.retry_action(lambda: self.run_with_epew(program, argument, working_directory, print_erro
 2111
 12112    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12113    def run_with_epew(self, program: str, argument: str = "", working_directory: str = None, print_errors_as_information
 02114        epew_argument:list[str]=["-p",program ,"-w", working_directory]
 02115        if encode_argument_in_base64:
 02116            if arguments_for_log is None:
 02117                argument_escaped=argument.replace("\"", "\\\"")
 02118                arguments_for_log=epew_argument+["-a",f"\"{argument_escaped}\""]
 02119            base64_string = base64.b64encode(argument.encode("utf-8")).decode("utf-8")
 02120            epew_argument=epew_argument+["-a",base64_string,"-b"]
 2121        else:
 02122            epew_argument=epew_argument+["-a",argument]
 02123            if arguments_for_log is None:
 02124                arguments_for_log=epew_argument
 02125        return self.run_program_argsasarray("epew", epew_argument, working_directory, print_errors_as_information, log_f
 2126
 2127
 2128    # </run programs>
 2129
 12130    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12131    def extract_archive_with_7z(self, unzip_program_file: str, zip_file: str, password: str, output_directory: str) -> N
 02132        password_set = not password is None
 02133        file_name = Path(zip_file).name
 02134        file_folder = os.path.dirname(zip_file)
 02135        argument = "x"
 02136        if password_set:
 02137            argument = f"{argument} -p\"{password}\""
 02138        argument = f"{argument} -o {output_directory}"
 02139        argument = f"{argument} {file_name}"
 02140        return self.run_program(unzip_program_file, argument, file_folder)
 2141
 12142    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12143    def get_internet_time(self) -> datetime:
 02144        response = ntplib.NTPClient().request('pool.ntp.org')
 02145        return datetime.fromtimestamp(response.tx_time)
 2146
 12147    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12148    def system_time_equals_internet_time(self, maximal_tolerance_difference: timedelta) -> bool:
 02149        return abs(GeneralUtilities.get_now() - self.get_internet_time()) < maximal_tolerance_difference
 2150
 12151    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12152    def system_time_equals_internet_time_with_default_tolerance(self) -> bool:
 02153        return self.system_time_equals_internet_time(self.__get_default_tolerance_for_system_time_equals_internet_time()
 2154
 12155    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12156    def check_system_time(self, maximal_tolerance_difference: timedelta):
 02157        if not self.system_time_equals_internet_time(maximal_tolerance_difference):
 02158            raise ValueError("System time may be wrong")
 2159
 12160    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12161    def check_system_time_with_default_tolerance(self) -> None:
 02162        self.check_system_time(self.__get_default_tolerance_for_system_time_equals_internet_time())
 2163
 12164    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12165    def __get_default_tolerance_for_system_time_equals_internet_time(self) -> timedelta:
 02166        return timedelta(hours=0, minutes=0, seconds=3)
 2167
 12168    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12169    def increment_version(self, input_version: str, increment_major: bool, increment_minor: bool, increment_patch: bool)
 02170        splitted = input_version.split(".")
 02171        GeneralUtilities.assert_condition(len(splitted) == 3, f"Version '{input_version}' does not have the 'major.minor
 02172        major = int(splitted[0])
 02173        minor = int(splitted[1])
 02174        patch = int(splitted[2])
 02175        if increment_major:
 02176            major = major+1
 02177        if increment_minor:
 02178            minor = minor+1
 02179        if increment_patch:
 02180            patch = patch+1
 02181        return f"{major}.{minor}.{patch}"
 2182
 12183    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12184    def get_semver_version_from_gitversion(self, repository_folder: str) -> str:
 02185        self.assert_is_git_repository(repository_folder)
 02186        if (self.git_repository_has_commits(repository_folder)):
 02187            result = self.get_version_from_gitversion(repository_folder, "MajorMinorPatch")
 02188            if self.git_repository_has_uncommitted_changes(repository_folder):
 02189                if self.get_current_git_branch_has_tag(repository_folder):
 02190                    id_of_latest_tag = self.git_get_commit_id(repository_folder, self.get_latest_git_tag(repository_fold
 02191                    current_commit = self.git_get_commit_id(repository_folder)
 02192                    current_commit_is_on_latest_tag = id_of_latest_tag == current_commit
 02193                    if current_commit_is_on_latest_tag:
 02194                        result = self.increment_version(result, False, False, True)
 2195        else:
 02196            result = "0.1.0"
 02197        return result
 2198
 12199    @staticmethod
 12200    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12201    def is_patch_version(version_string: str) -> bool:
 02202        return not version_string.endswith(".0")
 2203
 12204    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12205    def get_version_from_gitversion(self, folder: str, variable: str) -> str:
 2206        # called twice as workaround for issue 1877 in gitversion ( https://github.com/GitTools/GitVersion/issues/1877 )
 02207        result = self.run_program_argsasarray("gitversion", ["/showVariable", variable], folder)
 02208        result = self.run_program_argsasarray("gitversion", ["/showVariable", variable], folder)
 02209        result = GeneralUtilities.strip_new_line_character(result[1])
 2210
 02211        return result
 2212

 12222    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12223    def generate_certificate(self, folder: str,  domain: str, filename: str, subj_c: str, subj_st: str, subj_l: str, sub
 02224        if days_until_expire is None:
 02225            days_until_expire = 397
 02226        if password is None:
 02227            password = GeneralUtilities.generate_password()
 02228        rsa_key_length = 4096
 02229        self.run_program_argsasarray("openssl", ['genrsa', '-out', f'{filename}.key', f'{rsa_key_length}'], folder)
 02230        self.run_program_argsasarray("openssl", ['req', '-new', '-subj', f'/C={subj_c}/ST={subj_st}/L={subj_l}/O={subj_o
 02231        self.run_program_argsasarray("openssl", ['pkcs12', '-export', '-out', f'{filename}.selfsigned.pfx', '-password',
 02232        GeneralUtilities.write_text_to_file(os.path.join(folder, f"{filename}.password"), password)
 02233        GeneralUtilities.write_text_to_file(os.path.join(folder, f"{filename}.san.conf"), f"""[ req ]
 2234default_bits        = {rsa_key_length}
 2235distinguished_name  = req_distinguished_name
 2236req_extensions      = v3_req
 2237default_md          = sha256
 2238dirstring_type      = nombstr
 2239prompt              = no
 2240
 2241[ req_distinguished_name ]
 2242countryName         = {subj_c}
 2243stateOrProvinceName = {subj_st}
 2244localityName        = {subj_l}
 2245organizationName    = {subj_o}
 2246organizationUnit    = {subj_ou}
 2247commonName          = {domain}
 2248
 2249[v3_req]
 2250subjectAltName      = @subject_alt_name
 2251
 2252[ subject_alt_name ]
 2253DNS                 = {domain}
 2254""")
 2255

 02268        self.run_program_argsasarray("openssl", ['pkcs12', '-export', '-out', f'{filename}.pfx', f'-inkey', f'{filename}

 12270    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12271    def update_dependencies_of_python_in_requirementstxt_file(self, file: str, ignored_dependencies: list[str]):
 2272        # TODO consider ignored_dependencies
 02273        lines = GeneralUtilities.read_lines_from_file(file)
 02274        new_lines = []
 02275        for line in lines:
 02276            if GeneralUtilities.string_has_content(line):
 02277                new_lines.append(self.__get_updated_line_for_python_requirements(line.strip()))
 02278        GeneralUtilities.write_lines_to_file(file, new_lines)
 2279
 12280    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12281    def __get_updated_line_for_python_requirements(self, line: str) -> str:
 02282        if "==" in line or "<" in line:
 02283            return line
 02284        elif ">" in line:
 02285            try:
 2286                # line is something like "cyclonedx-bom>=2.0.2" and the function must return with the updated version
 2287                # (something like "cyclonedx-bom>=2.11.0" for example)
 02288                package = line.split(">")[0]
 02289                operator = ">=" if ">=" in line else ">"
 02290                headers = {'Cache-Control': 'no-cache'}
 02291                response = requests.get(f'https://pypi.org/pypi/{package}/json', timeout=5, headers=headers)
 02292                latest_version = response.json()['info']['version']
 2293                # TODO update only minor- and patch-version
 2294                # TODO print info if there is a new major-version
 02295                return package+operator+latest_version
 02296            except:
 02297                return line
 2298        else:
 02299            raise ValueError(f'Unexpected line in requirements-file: "{line}"')
 2300
 12301    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12302    def update_dependencies_of_python_in_setupcfg_file(self, setup_cfg_file: str, ignored_dependencies: list[str]):
 2303        # TODO consider ignored_dependencies
 02304        lines = GeneralUtilities.read_lines_from_file(setup_cfg_file)
 02305        new_lines = []
 02306        requirement_parsing_mode = False
 02307        for line in lines:
 02308            new_line = line
 02309            if (requirement_parsing_mode):
 02310                if ("<" in line or "=" in line or ">" in line):
 02311                    updated_line = f"    {self.__get_updated_line_for_python_requirements(line.strip())}"
 02312                    new_line = updated_line
 2313                else:
 02314                    requirement_parsing_mode = False
 2315            else:
 02316                if line.startswith("install_requires ="):
 02317                    requirement_parsing_mode = True
 02318            new_lines.append(new_line)
 02319        GeneralUtilities.write_lines_to_file(setup_cfg_file, new_lines)
 2320
 12321    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12322    def update_dependencies_of_dotnet_project(self, csproj_file: str,  ignored_dependencies: list[str]):
 02323        folder = os.path.dirname(csproj_file)
 02324        csproj_filename = os.path.basename(csproj_file)
 02325        self.log.log(f"Check for updates in {csproj_filename}", LogLevel.Information)
 02326        result = self.run_program_with_retry("dotnet", f"list {csproj_filename} package --outdated", folder, print_error
 02327        for line in result[1].replace("\r", GeneralUtilities.empty_string).split("\n"):
 2328            # Relevant output-lines are something like "    > NJsonSchema             10.7.0        10.7.0      10.9.0"
 02329            if ">" in line:
 02330                package_name = line.replace(">", GeneralUtilities.empty_string).strip().split(" ")[0]
 02331                if not (package_name in ignored_dependencies):
 02332                    self.log.log(f"Update package {package_name}...", LogLevel.Debug)
 02333                    time.sleep(1.1)  # attempt to prevent rate-limit
 02334                    self.run_program_with_retry("dotnet", f"add {csproj_filename} package {package_name}", folder, print
 2335

 2339        # prepare

 02342        GeneralUtilities.ensure_directory_exists(temp_folder)

 02344        tool_content_folder_name = toolname+"Content"
 2345
 2346        # create folder
 02347        GeneralUtilities.ensure_directory_exists(temp_folder)
 02348        control_content_folder_name = "controlcontent"
 02349        packagecontent_control_folder = os.path.join(temp_folder, control_content_folder_name)
 02350        GeneralUtilities.ensure_directory_exists(packagecontent_control_folder)
 02351        data_content_folder_name = "datacontent"
 02352        packagecontent_data_folder = os.path.join(temp_folder, data_content_folder_name)
 02353        GeneralUtilities.ensure_directory_exists(packagecontent_data_folder)
 02354        entireresult_content_folder_name = "entireresultcontent"
 02355        packagecontent_entireresult_folder = os.path.join(temp_folder, entireresult_content_folder_name)
 02356        GeneralUtilities.ensure_directory_exists(packagecontent_entireresult_folder)
 2357
 2358        # create "debian-binary"-file
 02359        debianbinary_file = os.path.join(packagecontent_entireresult_folder, "debian-binary")
 02360        GeneralUtilities.ensure_file_exists(debianbinary_file)
 02361        GeneralUtilities.write_text_to_file(debianbinary_file, "2.0\n")
 2362
 2363        # create control-content
 2364
 2365        #  conffiles
 02366        conffiles_file = os.path.join(packagecontent_control_folder, "conffiles")
 02367        GeneralUtilities.ensure_file_exists(conffiles_file)
 2368
 2369        #  postinst-script
 02370        postinst_file = os.path.join(packagecontent_control_folder, "postinst")
 02371        GeneralUtilities.ensure_file_exists(postinst_file)
 02372        exe_file = f"/usr/bin/{tool_content_folder_name}/{toolname}"
 02373        link_file = f"/usr/bin/{toolname.lower()}"
 02374        permission = str(permission_of_executable_file_as_octet_triple)
 02375        GeneralUtilities.write_text_to_file(postinst_file, f"""#!/bin/sh
 2376ln -s {exe_file} {link_file}
 2377chmod {permission} {exe_file}
 2378chmod {permission} {link_file}
 2379""")
 2380
 2381        #  control
 02382        control_file = os.path.join(packagecontent_control_folder, "control")
 02383        GeneralUtilities.ensure_file_exists(control_file)
 02384        GeneralUtilities.write_text_to_file(control_file, control_file_content)
 2385
 2386        #  md5sums
 02387        md5sums_file = os.path.join(packagecontent_control_folder, "md5sums")
 02388        GeneralUtilities.ensure_file_exists(md5sums_file)
 2389
 2390        # create data-content
 2391
 2392        #  copy binaries
 02393        usr_bin_folder = os.path.join(packagecontent_data_folder, "usr/bin")
 02394        GeneralUtilities.ensure_directory_exists(usr_bin_folder)
 02395        usr_bin_content_folder = os.path.join(usr_bin_folder, tool_content_folder_name)
 02396        GeneralUtilities.copy_content_of_folder(bin_folder, usr_bin_content_folder)
 2397
 2398        # create debfile
 02399        deb_filename = f"{toolname}.deb"
 02400        self.run_program_argsasarray("tar", ["czf", f"../{entireresult_content_folder_name}/control.tar.gz", "*"], packa
 02401        self.run_program_argsasarray("tar", ["czf", f"../{entireresult_content_folder_name}/data.tar.gz", "*"], packagec
 02402        self.run_program_argsasarray("ar", ["r", deb_filename, "debian-binary", "control.tar.gz", "data.tar.gz"], packag
 02403        result_file = os.path.join(packagecontent_entireresult_folder, deb_filename)
 02404        shutil.copy(result_file, os.path.join(deb_output_folder, deb_filename))
 2405
 2406        # cleanup
 02407        GeneralUtilities.ensure_directory_does_not_exist(temp_folder)
 2408
 12409    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12410    def update_year_in_copyright_tags(self, file: str) -> None:
 02411        current_year = str(GeneralUtilities.get_now().year)
 02412        lines = GeneralUtilities.read_lines_from_file(file)
 02413        lines_result = []
 02414        for line in lines:
 02415            if match := re.search("(.*<[Cc]opyright>.*)\\d\\d\\d\\d(.*<\\/[Cc]opyright>.*)", line):
 02416                part1 = match.group(1)
 02417                part2 = match.group(2)
 02418                adapted = part1+current_year+part2
 2419            else:
 02420                adapted = line
 02421            lines_result.append(adapted)
 02422        GeneralUtilities.write_lines_to_file(file, lines_result)
 2423
 12424    @GeneralUtilities.check_arguments
 12425    def update_year_in_first_line_of_file(self, file: str) -> None:
 02426        current_year = str(GeneralUtilities.get_now().year)
 02427        lines = GeneralUtilities.read_lines_from_file(file)
 02428        lines[0] = re.sub("\\d\\d\\d\\d", current_year, lines[0])
 02429        GeneralUtilities.write_lines_to_file(file, lines)
 2430
